title: tirucalia gregaria
intro: Tirucalia gregaria, also known as the 'false saffron' or 'yellow root', is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Tirucalia gregaria include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be made by steeping the roots and rhizomes in hot water.
